# nable

> Local-first, AI-native FinOps. Ask anything about your cloud and AI spend, right inside Claude, Cursor, or any MCP editor. nable runs on your own machine, connects to AWS, Azure, GCP and 10+ SaaS and AI providers, and your credentials never leave your laptop.

nable is an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server you install locally. It answers cost questions, detects anomalies, recommends rightsizing, tracks AI and LLM spend by model (Amazon Bedrock, OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI), and drafts the pull request to fix waste. There is no SaaS account to create and no nable backend that holds your data. The differentiator is the intersection: genuinely multi-cloud, plus cross-provider AI spend, plus true local-first, in one answer.

## What nable does
- Answer cloud and AI cost questions across AWS, Azure, GCP, OpenAI, Anthropic, Bedrock and more
- Detect cost anomalies against a rolling baseline and alert in Slack or Teams
- Generate rightsizing recommendations, then draft the Terraform fix as a pull request for human approval
- Open Jira, Linear, or GitHub tickets for waste over a threshold
- Track LLM spend by model and provider, with cost per token and per customer
- Attribute AI coding output to the model that wrote it: cost per pull request and per commit, by model
- Preflight a Terraform or Helm change before you apply it, with an ok / warn / over-budget verdict against your budget
- Attribute spend to teams and services

## Propose-only by design
nable proposes; a human decides. It drafts pull requests, opens tickets, and raises alerts. It never edits, deletes, or buys anything in your cloud on its own. Every change is reviewed and applied by a person.

## Why it is different
- Local-first: it runs on your machine. Credentials stay in your OS keyring, cost data caches in a local SQLite database, nothing is shipped to a vendor backend.
- AI-native: it lives in Claude or Cursor, not a separate dashboard you have to log into.
- Cross-cloud plus AI spend in one answer. Token prices keep falling, but agents use 5 to 30 times more tokens, so AI bills keep climbing. nable shows exactly why.

## Key pages
- [Home](https://getnable.com/): what nable is, how it works, live demo, FAQ
- [Docs](https://getnable.com/docs): install and setup, every tool, connector guides, the full capability map
- [Pricing](https://getnable.com/pricing.md): plans and prices, machine-readable
- [Security](https://getnable.com/security): architecture, credential handling, AI exposure, disclosure policy
- [Access scope](https://getnable.com/iam): exact least-privilege read-only IAM policies for AWS, GCP, Azure; what nable can and cannot touch
- [FinOps MCP comparison](https://getnable.com/finops-mcp-comparison): nable vs AWS Billing MCP, Vantage, Finout and open-source servers, compared honestly
- [About](https://getnable.com/about): who builds nable and why

## Install
uvx nable

## Pricing summary
- Dev: $0, no credit card, no expiry. Cost queries, anomaly detection, rightsizing, LLM spend tracking, every connector. Bring your own LLM key.
- Pro: $100 per month, flat for the whole team, not per seat. Adds Terraform remediation pull requests, ticket creation, Slack and Teams alerts, digests, budgets, commitment recommendations, the conversational Slack bot, and root-cause analysis.
- Startups: $1,000 per month flat. Everything in Pro, plus single-tenant hosting we run for you and a managed AI agent billed at provider cost with no markup, usage metered above an included allowance and hard-capped.
